bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting my maximum-utility bid. The opponent opened with all valueE, which is the lowest utility for me on every issue and provides no evidence yet of willingness to trade off, so I will anchor on my strongest valid offer.

The opponent has moved from an all-valueE offer to valueD on the two most important issues, raising my utility from 0.20 to 0.32, so they are conceding but still far from a strong bid for me. Since it is very early at t=0.02, I am targeting a very high-utility offer while making a small concession on a low-weight issue to avoid simply repeating my prior bid.

I am targeting a very high utility bid (about 0.96) because it is still extremely early at t=0.04 and the opponent has already improved their offers to me from all valueE to valueC on the two highest-weight issues. Their behavior suggests some concession is happening, so I will hold firm on my most important issues A, B, and C while making small concessions on low-weight issues D and E to avoid repeating my last bid.

The opponent’s offers have been steadily improving for me, rising from very low utility to about 0.60 by t=0.06, so I read them as conceding rather than holding firm. Since it is still very early, I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 while making further concessions only on the lowest-weight issues, issueD and issueE, which the opponent appears to value differently.

I am targeting about 0.88 utility: holding firm on the high-weight issues A and B and also keeping issueC at my best value, while conceding further on lower-weight issues D and E. Since it is still very early at t=0.08, I should not drop too far, but the opponent’s offers show clear concession on issueA and issueB while they continue to hold issueD and issueE low for me, so I will trade on those lower-weight issues first.

I am targeting a still-strong utility of 0.84 by holding firm on the highest-weight issues A, B, and C while conceding the low-weight issues D and E. At t=0.10 there is little deadline pressure, but the opponent’s offers have steadily improved for me and they consistently keep issueD and issueE at valueE, suggesting those are good concession points.

I am targeting about 0.80 utility, conceding moderately because it is still early but the opponent has clearly improved from all valueE to holding issueA and issueB at my top values. Their latest repeated offer gives me 0.76, so I will hold firm on the high-weight issues A and B while moving issueC closer to their position and leaving low-weight D/E conceded.

I am targeting a utility around 0.82, still strong because we are early at t=0.14 and there is no need to rush to their standing offer yet. The opponent has conceded substantially on high-weight issueA and issueB but is holding issueC at valueC and issueD/issueE at valueE, so I keep my main demand on issueC while making only a small variation from my prior bid to avoid exact repetition.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.80 by holding firm on the high-importance issues A and B, keeping issueC at valueB, and conceding the low-weight issues D and E. Although it is still early at t=0.16, the opponent’s offers have steadily improved for me and their latest bid exactly signals willingness around this package, so mirroring it is a strong agreement-seeking move without sacrificing the core issues.
